query: History of Barcelona's Roman roots and where to see them today

result.query: History of Barcelona's Roman roots and where to see them today
result.answer: The MUHBA Barcelona History Museum is located at Plaça del Rei, featuring Roman ruins from the ancient city of Barcino. The museum is in the Gothic Quarter, accessible via the "Catedral - Gòtic" metro stop.

answer:
**MUHBA Plaça del Rei (Museum of the History of Barcelona)**
- Location/Transit: Nearest metro is "Catedral - Gòtic" stop; also served by hop-on hop-off bus
- Address: Plaça del Rei, s/n, 08002 Barcelona (Gothic Quarter)
- Hours/Schedule: Tuesday 10:00-19:00, Wednesday-Sunday 10:00-19:00, Monday closed [Confidence: MEDIUM — based on Yelp listing]
- Price: Free with Barcelona Card; standard admission — unavailable —
- Why It Fits: The main museum showcasing Barcino — the 2,000-year-old Roman settlement. Underground excavations include preserved Roman streets, city walls, a 2nd-century laundry facility, and Visigothic remains. This is the most comprehensive Roman site in Barcelona.
- Confidence: HIGH — multiple authoritative sources confirm this as the primary Roman heritage site

**Roman Walls of Barcelona**
- Location/Transit: In Gothic Quarter, surrounding the historic center; walkable from Plaça del Rei
- Address: Various sections along Carrer del Sotstinent Navarro, Carrer de la Ciutat
- Hours/Schedule: Visible 24/7 from street level; some sections accessible within MUHBA complex
- Price: Free (exterior viewing)
- Why It Fits: The Roman walls that surrounded Barcino from the 1st century BC to the Middle Ages still embrace the heart of Barcelona. The best-preserved sections are near the cathedral and along Carrer del Sotstinent Navarro.
- Confidence: HIGH — confirmed by Barcelona City Council official source

**Roman Necropolis (Plaça de la Mercè)**
- Location/Transit: Nearest metro Jaume I (L4); 5-minute walk from Gothic Quarter
- Address: Plaça de la Mercè, 08002 Barcelona
- Hours/Schedule: — unavailable —
- Price: — unavailable —
- Why It Fits: One of the few visible Roman necropolises in Barcelona, dating from the 1st-3rd century AD. Provides insight into Roman burial practices on the outskirts of ancient Barcino.
- Confidence: MEDIUM — mentioned in ForeverBarcelona guide

**Roman Baths (Banys Romans)**
- Location/Transit: In Gothic Quarter, near Carrer del Bisbe
- Address: Carrer del Bisbe, 08002 Barcelona
- Hours/Schedule: — unavailable —
- Price: — unavailable —
- Why It Fits: Remains of Roman thermal baths discovered beneath the Gothic Quarter, demonstrating the urban amenities of ancient Barcino.
- Confidence: LOW — limited specific details available in sources

**Key Practical Note**: The MUHBA Plaça del Rei is the essential visit — it contains the most extensive underground Roman ruins walkable through preserved streets of Barcino, including the city wall, residential areas, and early Christian community remains. Allow 1.5-2 hours for the underground section.

sources.title: Barcelona History Museum(MUHBA): Journey Through Time and Unravel the City's Rich Past
sources.snippet: # Delve into Barcelona's Intriguing Past at the Barcelona History Museum (MUHBA). And if you're keen to delve even deeper into the city's intriguing past, the Barcelona History Museum (MUHBA) is the perfect destination for you. The Barcelona History Museum is a place where you can learn about the cultural history of Barcelona city. ## Location map showing the walking routes to the Barcelona History Museum from the nearest metro stops. Location map of Barcelona History Museum in Barcelona, Spain. Below you can find the address, contact telephone, nearest metro station, parking, opening hours, entrance fee and the link to the official website of the Barcelona History Museum. ## How to get to Barcelona History Museum. Nearest stop for Barcelona History Museum is **"Catedral - Gòtic"** with the  "hop on hop off" sightseeing bus. Car Park Car parking near Barcelona History Museum close to Barcelona Cathedral. **Website:** Barcelona History Museum MUHBA.

sources.title: Plaça del Rei | Museu Historia de Barcelona | Ajuntament de Barcelona
sources.snippet: Logo of Barcelona City Council. # Plaça del Rei. The monumental complex of Plaça del Rei has been the founding core of the Barcelona History Museum since its creation in 1943. The remains included in the monumental complex of Plaça del Rei include Roman *Barcino* from the 1st century BC, Visigothic *Barchinona* from the 7th century AD and medieval Barcelona from the 13th century. Visitors to this underground area can walk along the streets of Roman Barcelona, past the city wall; they can look around a laundry from the 2nd century A.D. and see the remains of the city's first Christian community, as well as other spaces. This complex also includes Casa Padellàs, a former palace from the early 16th century that was moved, almost a hundred years ago, from Carrer Mercaders to Plaça del Rei. Converted into the Casa de la Història, it is an experimental museum space, a project with different formats that addresses Barcelona through urban history and heritage in an environment of research and participatory activities.

sources.title: Museum of Urban History of Barcelona
sources.snippet: You are here:  barcelona.de > Attractions > Museums > Història de la Ciutat. # Museu d'Història de la Ciutat (MUHBA) - Museum of Urban History. The Museu d 'Història de la Ciutat (City History Museum) in the Gothic district tells you the history of the city of Barcelona since Roman times. You get free admission to the Museu d'Història de la Cuitat with the Barcelona Card. At the beginning of the museum tour you will drive down to the excavations of Barcino, the Roman city of Barcelona.
